Notes.Text:
This update for containerd fixes the following issues:

- CVE-2024-40635: Fixed integer overflow in User ID handling (bsc#1239749) 
  
Other fixes:
- Update to containerd v1.7.27.

Notes.Title: Patchnames

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Containers 15 SP6
SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Containers 15 SP6
SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Containers 15 SP6

Vulnerabilities.Description: containerd is an open-source container runtime. A bug was found in containerd prior to versions 1.6.38, 1.7.27, and 2.0.4 where containers launched with a User set as a `UID:GID` larger than the maximum 32-bit signed integer can cause an overflow condition where the container ultimately runs as root (UID 0). This could cause unexpected behavior for environments that require containers to run as a non-root user. This bug has been fixed in containerd 1.6.38, 1.7.27, and 2.04. As a workaround, ensure that only trusted images are used and that only trusted users have permissions to import images.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Module for Containers 15 SP6:containerd-1.7.27-150000.123.1
